title: Peppermint Spice Steak
description: A peppery, light, adventurous Saudi Arabian dish featuring beef cooked to perfection with a touch of nut-free-friendly mint and spice blend.

ingredients.name: Beef Steak
ingredients.notes: Choose a meaty cut of beef like sirloin or ribeye.

ingredients.name: Peppermint Essence
ingredients.amount: 2 tsp (optional but recommended for the mint flavor)
ingredients.notes: For those who prefer a nut-free-friendly option, use this instead of traditional peppermint extract.

ingredients.name: All-Purpose Flour
ingredients.notes: To coat and help with browning.

ingredients.name: Vegetable Oil
ingredients.notes: For cooking and searing the steak on a pan or grill.

ingredients.name: Cumin Powder
ingredients.notes: To enhance the meat's natural flavors and provide a warm, earthy note.

ingredients.name: Coriander Powder
ingredients.notes: Another warming spice that complements well with cumin and adds a slight citrusy edge to the dish.

ingredients.name: Salt and Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Adjust seasoning based on personal preference for saltiness and spiciness.

instructions: Preheat your grill or pan over medium-high heat.
Season both sides of the steak with a pinch of salt and pepper. If using, mix peppermint essence into flour until fully incorporated.
Coat the steak evenly with the flour mixture to seal in juices and add texture.
Heat the oil in a pan or on your grill to medium-high heat (around 375°F). Place the seasoned steak onto the hot surface.
Sear each side of the steak for about 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown. For grilling, ensure you get good searing time and char marks to enhance the flavor.
Place the steak back into the pan or grill and cook through (until internal temperature reaches 145°F/65°C for medium-rare). Cooking time will vary based on cut of meat.
Once cooked, let the steak rest for at least 5 minutes before slicing. This allows the juices to redistribute evenly throughout the meat.
Serve with a side of your favorite steamed vegetables and garnish with fresh herbs like parsley or mint if using peppermint essence.
